pattern: The same digit-folding process is really Pascal's triangle mod 10, so express each final digit as a sum of the original digits weighted by binomial coefficients, computed mod 2 and mod 5 and recombined.
hints: Each of the two final digits is a fixed linear combination of the original digits with binomial-coefficient weights -- the string is far too long to simulate the folding one pass at a time.
A binomial coefficient mod a small prime can be computed digit-by-digit in that prime's own base (Lucas' theorem), which stays fast even when the top row of the triangle is enormous.
Get each weight mod 2 and mod 5 separately, then recombine the two results into a single value mod 10 (there are only 10 candidates to check) before summing.

edge_inputs.description: minimum length, three digits, folds once
all identical digits stay identical through every fold
a long string (800 digits) well beyond what direct simulation would be practical for at full scale
carries wrap through 0 at several steps
four digits where the two results differ only after the final fold

stress.description: many random digit strings near the maximum length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(100000, size if size >= 3 else 3))
    s = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
